100 - Small Wooden Block - Right outer-south top z=6 - top of Block 99

Tower rings and diagonals (connectors; no volume)

B3 - Brace - Left tower z=1 square ring - connects 43↔51↔65↔58↔43 - function: horizontal frame  
B4 - Brace - Left tower z=3 square ring - connects 45↔53↔67↔60↔45 - function: horizontal frame  
B5 - Brace - Left tower z=5 square ring - connects 47↔55↔69↔62↔47 - function: horizontal frame

B6 - Brace - Left tower inner face X (portal) - connects 42↔56 and 50↔48 - function: tall pointed inner portal toward deck  
B7 - Brace - Left tower outer face X (portal) - connects 57↔70 and 64↔63 - function: tall pointed outer portal  
B8 - Brace - Left tower north face X - connects 42↔63 and 57↔48 - function: lateral stiffening  
B9 - Brace - Left tower south face X - connects 50↔70 and 64↔56 - function: lateral stiffening

B10 - Brace - Right tower z=1 square ring - connects 73↔81↔95↔88↔73 - function: horizontal frame  
B11 - Brace - Right tower z=3 square ring - connects 75↔83↔97↔90↔75 - function: horizontal frame  
B12 - Brace - Right tower z=5 square ring - connects 77↔85↔99↔92↔77 - function: horizontal frame

B13 - Brace - Right tower inner face X (portal) - connects 72↔86 and 80↔78 - function: tall pointed inner portal  
B14 - Brace - Right tower outer face X (portal) - connects 87↔100 and 94↔93 - function: tall pointed outer portal  
B15 - Brace - Right tower north face X - connects 72↔93 and 87↔78 - function: lateral stiffening  
B16 - Brace - Right tower south face X - connects 80↔100 and 94↔86 - function: lateral stiffening

Step C – Upper tie-walkway (braces only)

B17 - Brace - Top tie (north, between inner-north tops) - connects 48↔78 - function: crown tie  
B18 - Brace - Top tie (south, between inner-south tops) - connects 56↔86 - function: crown tie  
B19 - Brace - Top tie diagonal - connects 48↔86 - function: side lattice  
B20 - Brace - Top tie diagonal - connects 56↔78 - function: side lattice

Step D – Rope suspension (winches) and scaffold removal

W1 - Winch - Left inner-north top to west edge outermost - connects 48 to 17 (x=−5,y=+1) - function: static-length stay; set for slight sag toward midspan  
W2 - Winch - Left inner-north top to west edge mid - connects 48 to 15 (x=−3,y=+1) - function: static-length stay  
W3 - Winch - Left inner-north top to west edge inboard - connects 48 to 13 (x=−1,y=+1) - function: static-length stay

W4 - Winch - Left inner-south top to west edge outermost - connects 56 to 19 (x=−5,y=−1) - function: static-length stay  
W5 - Winch - Left inner-south top to west edge mid - connects 56 to 21 (x=−3,y=−1) - function: static-length stay  
W6 - Winch - Left inner-south top to west edge inboard - connects 56 to 23 (x=−1,y=−1) - function: static-length stay

W7 - Winch - Right inner-north top to east edge outermost - connects 78 to 7 (x=+5,y=+1) - function: static-length stay  
W8 - Winch - Right inner-north top to east edge mid - connects 78 to 9 (x=+3,y=+1) - function: static-length stay  
W9 - Winch - Right inner-north top to east edge inboard - connects 78 to 11 (x=+1,y=+1) - function: static-length stay

W10 - Winch - Right inner-south top to east edge outermost - connects 86 to 29 (x=+5,y=−1) - function: static-length stay  
W11 - Winch - Right inner-south top to east edge mid - connects 86 to 27 (x=+3,y=−1) - function: static-length stay  
W12 - Winch - Right inner-south top to east edge inboard - connects 86 to 25 (x=+1,y=−1) - function: static-length stay

W13 - Winch - Top “main cable” north - connects 48↔78 - function: crown line; lateral tie  
W14 - Winch - Top “main cable” south - connects 56↔86 - function: crown line; lateral tie

Scaffold removal note: After W1–W14 and B17–B20 are installed and tensioned, remove Blocks 41, 49, 71, and 79 (temporary scaffold stubs). This severs all rigid deck–tower block contacts so the deck is supported exclusively by ropes; towers remain rigid via upper ties and tower bracing.

Step E – Ornaments (functional blocks)

101 - Powered Wheel - Left tower rose window on inner face - east face of Block 45 (left inner-north post z=3) - vertical wheel facing east toward the deck; auto-braked; controls unassigned (decorative only) - function: spins at 100 rpm but unused; purely ornamental

102 - Powered Wheel - Right tower rose window on inner face - west face of Block 75 (right inner-north post z=3) - vertical wheel facing west toward the deck; auto-braked; controls unassigned (decorative only) - function: spins at 100 rpm but unused; purely ornamental

103 - Torch - Left tower north finial - top face of Block 48; oriented upward and slightly north - function: decorative light; flame sphere radius 0.3 above the tip; placed >1 unit from any water cannon  
104 - Torch - Left tower south finial - top face of Block 56; oriented upward and slightly south - function: decorative light; flame sphere radius 0.3; safe clearances maintained  
105 - Torch - Right tower north finial - top face of Block 78; oriented upward and slightly north - function: decorative light; flame sphere radius 0.3; safe clearances maintained  
106 - Torch - Right tower south finial - top face of Block 86; oriented upward and slightly south - function: decorative light; flame sphere radius 0.3; safe clearances maintained

107 - Water Cannon - Left tower gargoyle spout - north face of Block 62 (left outer-north post z=5), pointing +y - function: decorative only; leave unassigned so it never fires; >1 unit from torches prevents any heating/steam mode  
108 - Water Cannon - Right tower gargoyle spout - south face of Block 99 (right outer-south post z=5), pointing −y - function: decorative only; leave unassigned; >1 unit from torches prevents any heating/steam mode

Functional summary and final compliance
- Deck: 11×3 grid centered on Stone Base; validated geometry with no overlaps.  
- Towers: Inner posts at x=±7 and outer posts at x=±8; no rigid contact with deck after scaffold removal; ring and X bracing provide rigidity and Gothic portals.  
- Upper tie-walkway: Two horizontal braces plus two diagonals across tower tops; locks spacing.  
- Suspension: After removing Blocks 41, 49, 71, 79, the only tower–deck connections are winches W1–W12; deck hangs with adjustable, static-length sag.  
- Ornaments: Powered wheels unassigned (stationary visually), torches clear of cannons (heating radius 0.3), water cannons unassigned and oriented outward along ±y.

Position adjustment note (minor, deck centerline x=−1): Block 36 is specified “west of Block 34”; in practice, attach 36 to the west face of Block 1 (Stone Base) to realize the intended x=−1 center tile without attempting to traverse through the occupied x=0 tile. Functional placement remains identical.
<end blueprint>

title: builder thought
summary: Introduction to tools I will use to build your structure step by step I will construct your bridge exactly per your blueprint, one block or connector at a time, using the following tools. Please note: the note field on…
body:
Introduction to tools I will use to build your structure step by step

I will construct your bridge exactly per your blueprint, one block or connector at a time, using the following tools. Please note: the note field on each operation is very important; I will use it to record the specific function and identity of each block or connector so we can reliably reference them later.

- start: Begins the build by placing the starter block (Block 1). Inputs:
  - init_shift [x, y, z]: initial position offset (defaults to [0, 0, 0]).
  - init_rotation [yaw, pitch, roll]: initial rotation (defaults to [0, 0, 0]).
  - note: description of the machine or Block 1’s intended role. I will use this to mark “Block 1 – Stone Base – deck datum.”
- attach_block_to: Attaches a new block to a specific face of an existing block. Allowed new_block types: Small Wooden Block, Powered Wheel, Torch, Water Cannon. Inputs:
  - base_block: the existing block’s ID.
  - face: face label to attach on. I will map blueprint phrases to faces as follows: “east of” → face "East", “west of” → "West", “north of” → "North", “south of” → "South", “top of” → "Top", and if needed “bottom of” → "Bottom".
  - new_block: the block type per blueprint.
  - note: I’ll record the exact identity (e.g., “Block 7 – Deck top row (x=+5,y=+1)”).
  If any face availability is uncertain, I will use get_block_details first.
- connect_blocks: Creates non‑volumetric connections between two faces using connectors. Allowed connectors: Brace, Winch. Inputs:
  - block_a, face_a and block_b, face_b: endpoints and faces to connect.
  - connector: Brace or Winch.
  - note: I’ll record the connection’s identity and function (e.g., “B3 – Left tower z=1 ring” or “W1 – Left inner-north top to west edge outermost”).
  If required, I can confirm faces via get_block_details to avoid proximity conflicts.
- get_block_details: Returns full details (position, rotation, face info) for a block. I’ll use this to verify coordinates, orientations, and which faces are attachable when precision matters.
- get_machine_summary: Provides the latest overall state of the machine (overview without face captions). I will use this for periodic sanity checks between steps and it is mandatory to invoke for a final check before we conclude a build stage or the entire process. I will remind you before we finalize any phase.
- shift_block: Shifts a placed block by a small offset vector [x, y, z] within [-0.5, 0.5] each, useful if an attachment overlaps or needs micro‑adjustment after placement while maintaining attachment.
- twist_block: Twists a block around its rooted face (in degrees). Helpful for orienting directional blocks (e.g., ensuring a Torch or Water Cannon points the intended direction, or adjusting a wheel’s facing).
- flip_spin: Flips spin direction of a spinning block (e.g., Powered Wheel) if needed for aesthetics or symmetry.
- remove_block: Removes a block by its ID; I will use this in Step D to delete the four temporary scaffold stubs (Blocks 41, 49, 71, 79) after all winches and upper braces are installed and tensioned.
- reset: Resets the entire machine. Not expected to be used unless we intentionally restart.

Build plan alignment with your blueprint

- I will follow your steps A through E exactly, honoring the position adjustment notes (major tower shift and the minor deck centerline note for Block 36).
- One operation per message (one new block or connector at a time). I will label each operation with the blueprint ID and use the note field to encode the identity for easy traceability.
- Orientation and faces: I’ll use the cardinal/vertical mapping given above so “east of Block N” equals attach face "East" of Block N, and so on.

Proceeding with Step A – Start and place Block 1

I will initialize the machine and place Block 1 (Stone Base) at the center of the deck grid as the reference datum. After that, I’ll proceed to Block 2 (Deck centerline x=+1, east of Block 1).

Executing: Place Block 1 – Stone Base (starter 1×1×1) – Central deck datum.

Instruction:
Skip the creation of Block 30 because the intended location (x=+5, y=0) is already occupied by Block 6 from the earlier center row. Continue Step A by attaching a Small Wooden Block (designate as Block 31) to the west face of Block 6 (face center at [4.5, 0, 0], facing West). This places the center row tile at x=+4.

After executing, please report the new block’s ID, position, and its available attachable faces so I can guide the next placement.

title: builder message
summary: Acknowledged. I’m ready to attach “Block 31” as requested, but there’s a conflict to flag before executing: - The west face of Block 6 is already occupied by Block 5 at position x=+4, y=0. Accordingly, Block 6’s west fa…
body:
Acknowledged. I’m ready to attach “Block 31” as requested, but there’s a conflict to flag before executing:

- The west face of Block 6 is already occupied by Block 5 at position x=+4, y=0. Accordingly, Block 6’s west face is not available for a new attachment, and placing a new block there would overlap an existing deck tile.

Implication for Step A:
- The tiles for center row x=+4, +3, +2, +1 are already present as Blocks 5, 4, 3, and 2 respectively from the earlier center-row build. Thus, physical placements corresponding to blueprint labels 31–34 would duplicate existing tiles.

Recommended next action:
- Skip placements for the redundant center-row tiles (31–34) and proceed directly to the first new center-row tile not yet placed: Block 36 at x=−1, y=0. Per your minor note, we should attach it to the west face of Block 1 to realize x=−1 without traversing through the occupied x=0 tile.

Please confirm:
- Shall I proceed to place “Block 36 – Small Wooden Block – Deck center row x=−1 (y=0) – attach to the west face of Block 1” as the next operation? I will record the note accordingly.

title: guidance message
summary: Instruction: Proceed as suggested: attach a Small Wooden Block (designate as Block 36) to the west face (Face B) of Block 1 at face center [-0.5, 0, 0]. This places the center row tile at x=−1, y=0 per the minor positio…
body:
Instruction:
Proceed as suggested: attach a Small Wooden Block (designate as Block 36) to the west face (Face B) of Block 1 at face center [-0.5, 0, 0]. This places the center row tile at x=−1, y=0 per the minor position-adjustment note in Step A.

Notes:
- We will skip redundant center-row placements 30–34 since x=+1…+5 are already Blocks 2–6.
- After Block 36, we’ll continue west to place x=−2, −3, −4 (Blocks 37–39). We will not place 40 because x=−5, y=0 is already occupied by Block 18.

After executing, please report the new block’s ID, position, and available attachable faces so I can guide the next placement.

title: guidance message
summary: Instruction: Remove the final temporary scaffold Small Wooden Block with ID 72 (Block 79) at x=+6, y=−1, z=0. This severs the last rigid link between the deck’s bottom row east end (Block 29) and the right inner‑south t…
body:
Instruction:
Remove the final temporary scaffold Small Wooden Block with ID 72 (Block 79) at x=+6, y=−1, z=0. This severs the last rigid link between the deck’s bottom row east end (Block 29) and the right inner‑south tower base (Block 80), completing the Step D scaffold removal.

After executing, please confirm the removal succeeded and report any updates to neighboring blocks’ available faces (notably Block 29 and Block 80), so I can proceed with the right‑tower face X bracing (B13–B16).

title: guidance message
summary: Instruction: Install a Brace (B13 – Right tower inner face X, diagonal 1) connecting: - North face of Block 72 (ID 65, right inner‑north base) at approximately [7, 1.5, 0], to - South face of Block 86 (ID 79, right inne…
body:
Instruction:
Install a Brace (B13 – Right tower inner face X, diagonal 1) connecting:
- North face of Block 72 (ID 65, right inner‑north base) at approximately [7, 1.5, 0], to
- South face of Block 86 (ID 79, right inner‑south top) at approximately [7, −1.5, 6].

Notes:
- Using north/south faces avoids coincident face centers and keeps the brace on the tower’s inner plane.
- After this succeeds, we will add the crossing diagonal (B13, diagonal 2: Block 80 south face ↔ Block 78 north face) to complete the inner face X.

Please report success so I can provide the next brace placement.
